constraints.constraint: All enzymes must be shallow-copy safe (reassign entire fields, no nested mutation)
All config values must be read via substrate.cfg() every cycle — no constructor caching
No hardcoded thresholds, IDs, or magic numbers
Fail-safe: if config missing, use sensible default; never crash
DB writes are side-effects and cannot be rolled back — external calls first, substrate second
All indicator_history entries must represent real closed candles, not cycle snapshots

problem.title: confirmation_tf is dead weight for entry decisions
problem.description: The strategy YAML declares confirmation_tf (e.g., "1h") and describes entry confirmation via a second timeframe. CollectOHLCV fetches and computes indicators for both timeframes. However, ScoreConfluence and ValidateEntryZone only read primary_tf (list(sym_data.keys())[0]). The confirmation data is used only by DetectNoise (conflict checking) and RequestExit (signal reversal). Entry decisions are blind to it.
problem.current_behavior: score_confluence.py: primary_tf = list(sym_data.keys())[0] # ignores confirmation validate_entry_zone.py: primary_tf = list(sym_data.keys())[0] # ignores confirmation
problem.design_violation: The strategy config promises multi-timeframe confirmation but the reaction network does not enforce it. ISC-001 checks entry_threshold but not cross-timeframe alignment. This is a broken contract between config and execution.

requested_fix.approach: Require cross-timeframe alignment before a candidate is scored or validated

requirements.req: ScoreConfluence._score_timeframe() must score BOTH timeframes. The candidate's total score is only positive if BOTH timeframes agree in direction (both bullish or both bearish). If they conflict, the candidate is dropped or score is neutralized.
ValidateEntryZone must require confirmation_tf data to exist and align before producing an entry_zone. If confirmation_tf is null in config, skip this check (backward compatible).
Add an ISC or enzyme-level check: "confirmation_tf aligned with primary_tf direction for this candidate". If not aligned, the candidate is filtered before ApproveTrade sees it.
If confirmation_tf is removed from a strategy (set to null), the system must continue working without error. Do not require confirmation_tf for strategies that don't declare it.

problem.title: Trajectory analysis is destroyed by per-cycle snapshot spam
problem.description: CollectOHLCV appends one snapshot to indicator_history every cycle. With a 4h candle and 5-min cycle interval, the same forming candle produces 48 nearly-identical snapshots. The trajectory classifier (CollectPreTradeContext._classify_trajectory) counts "aligned bars" but these are not real bars — they are cycle snapshots of the same unfinished candle. After bootstrap, ISC-007 (coincidence_risk != high) almost never blocks trades because the artificial history looks "stable" even when the alignment was sudden.
problem.current_behavior: collect_ohlcv.py: history[symbol].append(snapshot) # EVERY CYCLE if len(history[symbol]) > lookback: history[symbol] = history[symbol][-lookback:]
problem.design_violation: The substrate stores cycle-count-based history (trajectory_lookback_bars: 12) but the underlying data source is candle-close-based. This is a unit mismatch: 12 cycle snapshots != 12 candle closes. The learning engine draws statistical conclusions from cycle noise, not market structure.

requested_fix.approach: Append snapshots only when a new candle has closed

requirements.req: CollectOHLCV must track the timestamp of the last closed candle per symbol per timeframe. Only append to indicator_history when the latest candle's close timestamp differs from the last stored timestamp. The forming (current) candle must NEVER produce a history entry.

Replace cycle-count-based configuration with time-based: - REMOVE: learning.trajectory_lookback_bars - REMOVE: learning.trajectory_min_bars - ADD: learning.trajectory_lookback_hours (default 48) - ADD: learning.trajectory_min_hours (default 8) The classifier counts real candles that fall within the lookback window, not a fixed number of snapshots.
The _bootstrap_indicator_history() method already computes from real historical bars. It must remain unchanged in principle but should respect the new time-based configuration.

On daemon restart, indicator_history starts empty. CollectOHLCV bootstraps from historical data. The first live append happens only when the NEXT candle closes after restart. Do NOT append the current forming candle just because it's the first cycle.
Store last_candle_close_ts in substrate.market (per symbol, per timeframe) so the check survives reset_cycle().

problem.title: retrain_every_hours exists in YAML but is never read
problem.description: Strategy YAMLs declare learning.retrain_every_hours (e.g., 12 or 6). UpdateRulebook.__init__ reads retrain_every_n_trades (default 10) and ignores the hours key entirely. Rulebook regeneration is trade-count-driven, which IS the correct trigger. The hours key is misleading dead config.
problem.design_violation: Occam's razor: a config key that is never read adds complexity without value. It creates false expectations about behavior.

requested_fix.approach: Remove the dead key and standardize on trade-count

requirements.req: Remove learning.retrain_every_hours from ALL strategy YAML files. The key has never been functional. Removing it eliminates confusion.
Keep learning.retrain_every_n_trades as the sole trigger. It is already config-driven and correct.
If a user adds retrain_every_hours to a custom strategy, the system must ignore it gracefully (no error, no effect).

problem.title: Learning parameters are hardcoded; YAML values are ignored
problem.description: The learning modules (analyzer.py, combination.py, trajectory.py, weight_adjuster.py) define DEFAULT_* constants that override or replace the strategy YAML values. UpdateLearning calls these functions without passing config-derived parameters. The result: paper_learning_test.yaml promises min_trades_before_adjusting: 15 but weight_adjuster.py uses DEFAULT_MIN_TRADES = 30. The learning engine is twice as conservative as the user configured.
problem.current_behavior: learning/analyzer.py: DEFAULT_MIN_TRADES_PER_SIGNAL = 15 learning/combination.py: DEFAULT_MIN_TRADES = 10 learning/trajectory.py: DEFAULT_MIN_TRADES = 15 learning/weight_adjuster.py: DEFAULT_MIN_TRADES = 30 enzymes/update_learning.py: update_signal_accuracy(strategy_name, strategy_uid=strategy_uid) # ^ min_trades_per_signal NOT passed compute_adjusted_weights(weight_map, strategy_name, strategy_uid=strategy_uid) # ^ min_trades NOT passed
problem.design_violation: Config-driven design requires that all tunable parameters live in config and are read every cycle. Hardcoded defaults violate the hot-reload contract: editing the YAML and reloading has no effect on learning behavior.

requested_fix.approach: Thread config values through the entire learning pipeline

requirements.req: All learning module functions must accept explicit parameters for thresholds instead of relying on module-level constants: - update_signal_accuracy(..., min_trades_per_signal: int) - update_combination_accuracy(..., min_trades: int, significance_level: float) - update_trajectory_accuracy(..., min_trades: int) - compute_adjusted_weights(..., min_trades: int)
The module-level DEFAULT_* constants become fallback defaults only when the parameter is explicitly omitted (backward compat for external callers). Internal callers must always pass values.

UpdateLearning.transform() must read ALL learning parameters from substrate.cfg() every cycle and pass them to the learning functions: - learning.min_trades_before_adjusting → compute_adjusted_weights - learning.min_trades_per_signal → update_signal_accuracy - learning.significance_level → update_combination_accuracy - learning.min_trades_per_signal → update_trajectory_accuracy
If a config key is missing, use the current hardcoded default as fallback. Log a debug message: "Using default X because config key Y is not set".

problem.title: Constructor caching prevents hot-reload from affecting enzyme behavior
problem.description: UpdateRulebook caches min_trades and retrain_every in __init__ from the config dict passed at daemon startup. When the daemon hot-reloads config (every cycle), the enzyme still uses the old cached values. The user edits the YAML, the daemon logs "Config changed after reload", but the enzyme's behavior does not change.
problem.current_behavior: update_rulebook.py: def __init__(self, config: dict | None = None): self._min_trades = 30 self._retrain_every = 10 if config: learning_cfg = config.get("learning", {}) self._min_trades = learning_cfg.get("min_trades_before_adjusting", 30) self._retrain_every = learning_cfg.get("retrain_every_n_trades", 10)
problem.design_violation: The substrate holds a reference to the live config (substrate._config) that is refreshed on every cycle. Enzymes must read from the substrate at decision time, not from a snapshot taken at construction time. Constructor caching is an anti-pattern in a hot-reload system.

requested_fix.approach: Read all tunable values from substrate.cfg() in can_activate() and transform()

requirements.req: UpdateRulebook.can_activate() must read min_trades and retrain_every from substrate.cfg() every time it is called. Remove self._min_trades and self._retrain_every instance variables entirely.
Audit ALL enzymes for constructor caching of config values. Any enzyme that stores config-derived thresholds in __init__ must be refactored to read from substrate.cfg() at runtime.
The config parameter passed to enzyme constructors should be treated as a bootstrap hint only, not a source of truth. The substrate is the source of truth for all cycle-level decisions.

problem.title: Max hold duration is recalculated every cycle for every position
problem.description: ApproveExit computes held_hours from opened_at on every cycle for every open position. opened_at never changes. max_hold_hours changes rarely. The calculation is pure waste: datetime parsing, arithmetic, and comparison run hundreds of times per day for no reason.
problem.current_behavior: approve_exit.py: opened_at = updated_pos.get("opened_at", "") if opened_at: opened_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(opened_at) held_hours = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - opened_dt).total_seconds() / 3600 if held_hours > max_hold_hours: should_exit = True
problem.design_violation: The reaction network should be efficient. Redundant computation on every cycle burns CPU and adds latency for no benefit. A value that is invariant for the lifetime of a position should be computed once and stored.

requested_fix.approach: Compute exit_deadline once at trade creation; compare once per cycle

requirements.req: ExecuteTrade.transform() must compute exit_deadline when creating the position dict: opened_dt = datetime.now(timezone.utc) max_hold_hours = substrate.cfg("exit_rules.max_hold_hours", 72) deadline = opened_dt + timedelta(hours=max_hold_hours) new_position["opened_at"] = opened_dt.isoformat() new_position["exit_deadline"] = deadline.isoformat()

If exit_deadline is missing (legacy positions from before this fix), fall back to the old opened_at calculation. Log a warning once per position: "Legacy position without exit_deadline, using opened_at fallback".

If the user hot-reloads max_hold_hours to a LOWER value, existing positions must respect their original deadline (no retroactive tightening). If the user increases max_hold_hours, existing positions do NOT get extended — the deadline is set at creation and is immutable. This prevents surprise exits after config edits.

problem.title: CollectOHLCV fetches full historical data every cycle even when nothing changed
problem.description: CollectOHLCV activates whenever market.indicators is empty. After the first cycle, indicators are populated, so theoretically it should not re-fire. However, the "freshness" check in can_activate is weak: it only checks if last_scan_at is empty. In practice, indicators are cleared by reset_cycle() every cycle, so CollectOHLCV fires every cycle, fetching 200 bars per symbol per timeframe regardless of whether a new candle has closed.
problem.current_behavior: collect_ohlcv.py can_activate(): indicators = substrate.market.get("indicators", {}) if not indicators: return True last_scan = substrate.market.get("last_scan_at", "") if not last_scan: return True return False # Only returns False if indicators exist AND last_scan is set # BUT reset_cycle() clears indicators every cycle!
problem.design_violation: The enzyme fetches public API data at cycle frequency, but the underlying data (4h candles) only changes every 4 hours. This is wasteful, risks rate limits, and fills the DB with identical cycle snapshots. The reaction network should distinguish between: - Signal data (slow: new candle closes) - Risk data (fast: mark prices for open positions)

requested_fix.approach: Smart activation: only fetch OHLCV when new data actually exists

requirements.req: CollectOHLCV.can_activate() must check three conditions: 1. FIRST CYCLE: indicators empty AND no last_candle_close_ts → activate 2. NEW CANDLE: current UTC time has crossed a timeframe boundary since last_candle_close_ts → activate 3. POSITIONS EXIST: open_positions not empty AND mark prices are stale (configurable staleness threshold, default 60s) → activate (but ONLY for mark prices, not full OHLCV recompute)

The indicator computation (RSI, MACD, EMA, ADX, ATR, S/R) must ONLY run when condition 1 or 2 is true. Condition 3 (positions exist) should trigger UpdateMarkPrices, not CollectOHLCV. Do NOT recompute indicators just because positions exist.
Store last_candle_close_ts per symbol per timeframe in substrate.market. Survive reset_cycle() by storing in a field that is NOT cleared by reset_cycle().

Add a helper function _should_refresh_ohlcv(timeframe, last_ts) that returns True when: - last_ts is empty (cold start) - floor(now, timeframe) > floor(last_ts, timeframe) Where floor() rounds a timestamp down to the start of the current candle for the given timeframe.
If CollectOHLCV skips fetching because no new candle closed, it must still preserve the existing indicators and indicator_history in the substrate. Do NOT clear them.

problem.title: Trajectory minimum history is checked in snapshots, not real time
problem.description: CollectPreTradeContext checks len(symbol_history) against trajectory_min_bars (default 4, not in YAML). With per-cycle snapshots, 4 snapshots = 20 minutes (5-min interval). After the fix in P2 (per-candle-close), 4 snapshots = 16 hours (4h candles). The threshold must be time-based to remain meaningful regardless of timeframe or interval configuration.
problem.current_behavior: collect_pre_trade_context.py: min_history = substrate.cfg("learning.trajectory_min_bars", 4) if len(symbol_history) < min_history: # block trade
problem.design_violation: Same as P2: unit mismatch between cycle snapshots and candle closes. After P2 is fixed, this check becomes too strict (16h minimum) or too loose depending on timeframe. Time-based thresholds are the only consistent approach.

requested_fix.approach: Use time-based thresholds and read from config every cycle

requirements.req: Replace trajectory_min_bars with trajectory_min_hours in config. CollectPreTradeContext reads this value from substrate.cfg() every cycle.
The history sufficiency check must compute the time span covered by the history entries (last timestamp - first timestamp) and compare against trajectory_min_hours. Do NOT count entries.

If history spans less than trajectory_min_hours, set coincidence_risk='high' and block via ISC-007. The log message must show real hours, not bar count: "Insufficient trajectory history for BTCUSDT: 3.2h / 8h required"

concern.title: Backward Compatibility
concern.description: All fixes must not break existing strategy YAMLs or databases. If a config key is renamed (e.g., trajectory_lookback_bars → trajectory_lookback_hours), the old key must still be accepted with a deprecation warning. If a new field is added to position dicts (e.g., exit_deadline), missing fields must be handled gracefully.

concern.title: Test Coverage
concern.description: Each fix must include unit tests: - P1: Test that conflicting confirmation_tf blocks entry - P2: Test that only candle-close appends to history - P4: Test that YAML min_trades values are respected - P5: Test that hot-reload changes enzyme behavior - P6: Test that exit_deadline is set and respected - P7: Test that CollectOHLCV skips when no new candle - P8: Test that time-based sufficiency check works

concern.title: Logging
concern.description: Every behavioral change (weight adjustment, rulebook regeneration, history append, skip decision) must be logged at INFO level with the config values that drove the decision. This enables debugging when behavior differs from expectations.
